title: Files, Descriptors, Locks, and RAII-Like C Patterns · Independent C Trial
summary: An independent maintainer ports the uniform resource-lifetime wrapper to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of uniform resource-lifetime wrapper with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.
Pair acquisition with one idempotent or guarded release operation and a valid empty state.
Use compiler cleanup attributes only behind a documented extension boundary with equivalent standard-C control flow.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource Not Memory
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 4476. Produce bounded C evidence to release nested resources once in reverse acquisition order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource Not Memory.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource Not Memory, keep Resource Not Memory connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

hints: For Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Composite Resource Object, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Composite Resource Object precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Composite Resource Object, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A lab native-systems team handles memory case 2592. Produce bounded C evidence to release nested resources once in reverse acquisition order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Composite Resource Object, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A structure assignment safely duplicates ownership of every nested handle.

stimulus.title: Composite Resource Object failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A structure assignment safely duplicates ownership of every nested handle.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Composite Resource Object condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Composite Resource Object case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Composite Resource Object
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Composite Resource Object: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 4925. Produce bounded C evidence to release nested resources once in reverse acquisition order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Composite Resource Object.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Composite Resource Object, keep Composite Resource Object connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.
